title: Segmentation Control Validation — Scanner IP Blocked at CDE Border as Expected

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 1.3.3 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 1.3.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.78: Segmentation Control Validation — Scanner IP Blocked at CDE Border as Expected.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.78' running on index=netfw pan:traffic with src in pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv ip AS src OUTPUT is_scanner
| lookup pci_cde_subnets.csv subnet AS dest OUTPUT in_cde
| where is_scanner="true" AND in_cde="true"
| stats count by action, rule, src, dest
| eval expected_deny=if(action IN ("deny","drop","reset-client"),1,0)
| stats sum(expected_deny) as deny_hits, count as total by src
| eval deny_ratio=if(total>0, round(100*deny_hits/total,2), null())
| where deny_ratio<95
| sort src
description: Confirms that active segmentation tests from non-CDE scanner IPs are denied at the NSC while still logged, producing evidence that untrusted networks cannot reach CHD without passing controls.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes netfw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Coordinate scanner IP list with annual segmentation test vendor. (2) Alert if `deny_ratio` drops (misconfiguration). (3) Attach results to PCI evidence library. (4) Run after major firewall pushes. Indexes required: `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`; Lookups: `pci_cde_subnets`, `pci_cde_subnets.csv`, `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips`, `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv`.
visualization: Table (src, deny_ratio), bar (action mix), single value (unexpected allows).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Scan failures during legacy system lifecycle, documented compensating controls, or planned upgrade windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` `pan:traffic` with `src` in `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Coordinate scanner IP list with annual segmentation test vendor. (2) Alert if `deny_ratio` drops (misconfiguration). (3) Attach results to PCI evidence library. (4) Run after major firewall pushes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv ip AS src OUTPUT is_scanner
| lookup pci_cde_subnets.csv subnet AS dest OUTPUT in_cde
| where is_scanner="true" AND in_cde="true"
| stats count by action, rule, src, dest
| eval expected_deny=if(action IN ("deny","drop","reset-client"),1,0)
| stats sum(expected_deny) as deny_hits, count as total by src
| eval deny_ratio=if(total>0, round(100*deny_hits/total,2), null())
| where deny_ratio<95
| sort src
```

Understanding this SPL

**Segmentation Control Validation — Scanner IP Blocked at CDE Border as Expected (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 1.3.3)** — Confirms that active segmentation tests from non-CDE scanner IPs are denied at the NSC while still logged, producing evidence that untrusted networks cannot reach CHD without passing controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `pan:traffic` with `src` in `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where is_scanner="true" AND in_cde="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by action, rule, src, dest** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **expected_deny** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deny_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where deny_ratio<95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Segmentation Control Validation — Scanner IP Blocked at CDE Border as Expected (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 1.3.3)** — Confirms that active segmentation tests from non-CDE scanner IPs are denied at the NSC while still logged, producing evidence that untrusted networks cannot reach CHD without passing controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `pan:traffic` with `src` in `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (src, deny_ratio), bar (action mix), single value (unexpected allows).
grandmaExplanation: We confirms that active segmentation tests from non-CDE scanner IPs are denied at the NSC while still logged, producing evidence that untrusted networks cannot reach CHD without passing controls. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
